title: hibbertia malleolacea
intro: Hibbertia malleolacea, also known as the Guinea flower, is a small, shrub-like plant native to Australia, belonging to the family Dilleniaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hibbertia malleolacea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Hibbertia malleolacea can be prepared as a tea or infused oil, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
